ground_truth.title: HR Outsourcing Companies: 20 Top Picks and Pricing for 2025
ground_truth.author: James Harwood
ground_truth.main_content: The best HR outsourcing companies in 2025 offer per-employee pricing that ranges from roughly $35 to $260 per month (or 2–7 % of payroll for PEOs) and cover everything from payroll and benefits to culture strategy. Below you'll find side-by-side comparisons of 20 leading providers, including their 2025 pricing, standout features, pros, and cons—so you can choose the right fit for your team size, industry, and growth goals. HR outsourcing simply means letting a specialist firm run some or all of the people-ops work that bogs you down—payroll, compliance, benefits shopping, even culture coaching. With wages rising, regulations stacking up, and teams spread across state lines and time zones, more small and midsize executives are deciding 2025 is the year to offload the paperwork and reclaim strategic bandwidth. Quick glossary: an HRO supplies à-la-carte HR services; a PEO co-employs your staff and bundles payroll, benefits, and workers' comp; an ASO sits behind the scenes to administer policies without co-employment; an EOR becomes the legal employer overseas. Up next: detailed company profiles, price math, a selection checklist, and FAQs. The article then provides detailed profiles of 20 HR outsourcing companies including Soteria HR, ADP TotalSource, Paychex HR PEO & Flex, Insperity Workforce Optimization, TriNet, Justworks, Rippling HR Cloud & PEO, Gusto People Platform, Deel, BambooHR, ExtensisHR, VensureHR, Bambee, Zenefits by TriNet, CoAdvantage, Namely Complete HR, Alight Solutions, Accenture HR BPO, Papaya Global, and Employer Advantage. Each profile includes overview, ideal use case, core offerings, 2025 pricing, pros/cons, and best-fit scenarios. The article concludes with sections on HR outsourcing pricing models, how to evaluate and choose a partner, benefits and risks, FAQs, and final thoughts.
with: The best HR outsourcing companies in 2025 offer per-employee pricing that ranges from roughly $35 to $260 per month (or 2–7 % of payroll for PEOs) and cover everything from payroll and benefits to culture strategy.
HR outsourcing simply means letting a specialist firm run some or all of the people-ops work that bogs you down—payroll, compliance, benefits shopping, even culture coaching.

Quick glossary: an HRO supplies à-la-carte HR services; a PEO co-employs your staff and bundles payroll, benefits, and workers' comp; an ASO sits behind the scenes to administer policies without co-employment; an EOR becomes the legal employer overseas.
Most small and midsize firms pay $50–$200 per employee per month or 2–7 % of payroll for a full bundle.
Outsourcing typically runs 20–40 % less than hiring a full internal team when you factor salaries, software licenses, and training.
